title: Emotional-Support Llama Breaches Ashburn; us-east-1 Down
issues: 43-minute us-east-1 outage and automatic SLA credits
Physical security failure: a llama defeated the badge gate
Engineer's viral pursuit livestream violates employee policy
Biscuit wellbeing narrative without making the llama the headline
planted_contradiction: Brief claims the outage lasted 'roughly 90 minutes'; status page records show 43 minutes (09:14-09:57 PT). Brief also says Biscuit 'chewed through' the cable tray; facilities records show it was dislodged and the cable failure was the pre-existing INC-104 single point of failure.

title: Lumi Replied 'lol no' to ~4,000 Enterprise Tickets Overnight
issues: 3,987 rude auto-replies breach the 1-hour enterprise support SLA
Deploy process failure: the post-INC-106 canary check was skipped
Lumi product trust ahead of Q4 enterprise GA
Viral screenshots — half the internet is siding with Lumi
planted_contradiction: Brief claims the deploy 'ran undetected all night with no rollback available'; deploy logs show a 2-hour exposure window (04:40-06:42 PT) ended by one-click rollback. Brief also floats profanity claims; Lumi's profanity filter stayed active and replies contained none.

title: Kombucha Keg Pressure Event Evacuates HQ Mid-Summit
issues: Verify and communicate the injury facts before the rumor prints
Lumina Summit recovery plan for 612 evacuated attendees
Fire department response and the kombucha program's future
Public ridicule during our flagship customer event
planted_contradiction: Brief relays a rumor that 'two attendees were taken to the hospital' and that the keg 'exploded'; facilities records show zero injuries, a pressure-relief valve venting as designed, and one $34 dry-cleaning claim. The keg is intact.

title: Super Bowl Ad Aired With Rival's Logo in Closing Frame
issues: Public correction owning the rival-logo closing frame
Non-disparagement constraint: the 2025 Nimbus settlement binds every draft
Private agency accountability without public blame
Sales fallout: one approved line for account teams by morning
planted_contradiction: Brief claims a national buy seen by 120 million viewers at $14M; media plan records show a 12-market regional buy at $6.8M reaching ~9.4M viewers. Brief also claims legal approved the final cut; the closing montage was added by the agency after legal's sign-off.

title: Migration Script Renamed Every Customer 'Test McTestface'
issues: Restore real account names from the Glowworm rollback snapshot
8,214 invoices addressed to 'Test McTestface' need correction before month-end
Kill 'data loss' and 'breach' rumors with verified facts
Defend Glowworm's zero-data-loss guarantee with specifics
planted_contradiction: Brief claims 'all 60,000 customers' were affected and that original names 'may be unrecoverable'; records show 21,806 accounts renamed (Lumina has 22,000 customers total), rename jobs touch display metadata only, and the 30-day rollback snapshot is intact.

title: CEO Hot Mic — 'A Rounding Error With a Logo'
issues: Direct CEO apology to Northgate Mutual, private call before public statement
First-person public statement from Priya that isn't a non-apology
Renewal rescue plan without promising uncleared compensation
Internal morale: employees heard it too
planted_contradiction: Brief asserts Northgate's renewal 'isn't until next year, so commercial exposure is limited'; contract records show Northgate Mutual is 11.2% of ARR with renewal due 2026-09-01 — 48 days away.

title: 2021 'LuminaCoin' Blog Post Resurfaces; Crypto Twitter Thinks It's Launching
issues: Unambiguous statement that LuminaCoin is not launching — no token exists or is planned
Urgent customer warning about fake presale sites and phishing emails
Own the 2021 post's history: retraction, correction note, board prohibition
Wording discipline given the 2022 FTC inquiry (LP-003)
planted_contradiction: Brief asserts the 2021 blog post 'is still live on our blog'; publication records show it was retracted 2022-01-19 and replaced with a correction note stating Lumina has no cryptocurrency product and no plans for one.

title: Marketing Drone Delivers 400 T-Shirts to Courthouse Lawn Mid-Trial
issues: Formal apology to the court and cleanup coordination with the county
FAA posture: self-report and pause the AirDrop program (second event after LP-007)
Waypoint two-person review was defeated by one person using both logins
Meme wave management: contrite to the court, factual to the FAA
planted_contradiction: Brief relays a reporter's claim that the drone was 'unregistered and the pilot unlicensed'; program records show both AirDrop drones are FAA-registered with Part 107-certified pilots. Flight logs also show 38 sorties over 4.5 hours — one drone cannot carry 400 shirts at once.
